 {"id":"bd-1nna","title":"Task: Create built-in webhook formatters for Slack, Discord, Teams","description":"## Overview
Implement message formatters that transform NTM events into platform-specific webhook payloads.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/webhook/formatters/:
   - Formatter interface: Format(event Event) ([]byte, error)
   - slack.go: Slack Block Kit formatting
   - discord.go: Discord embed formatting
   - teams.go: Microsoft Teams Adaptive Card formatting
   - json.go: Generic JSON (passthrough)

2. Slack formatter:
   - Use Block Kit for rich formatting
   - Color-code by event type (green=success, red=error)
   - Include actionable buttons where relevant
   - Format example:
     {
       "blocks": [
         {"type": "header", "text": {"type": "plain_text", "text": "Agent Completed"}},
         {"type": "section", "text": {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": "*myproject__cc_1* finished task"}}
       ],
       "attachments": [{"color": "good", ...}]
     }

3. Discord formatter:
   - Use embed objects for rich display
   - Color, title, description, fields, timestamp
   - Thumbnail for agent type icon

4. Teams formatter:
   - Adaptive Card format
   - Mention support for @user notifications
   - Action buttons for quick responses

## Implementation Notes
- Templates for each message type
- Variable substitution: {{.AgentName}}, {{.SessionID}}, etc.
- Escape special characters per platform

## Acceptance Criteria
- All formatters produce valid platform-specific payloads
- Messages look good in each platform
- Variables substitute correctly
- Unit tests for each formatter","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:38.327678526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:15.757668899Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nna","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nna","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nov","title":"Task: Add recovery config options","description":"## Purpose
Add configuration options for controlling session recovery behavior.

## Config Section
[recovery]
enabled = true                    # Master toggle
include_agent_mail = true         # Include recent messages
include_cm_memories = true        # Include procedural memories
include_beads_context = true      # Include BV task status
max_recovery_tokens = 2000        # Cap recovery context size
auto_inject_on_spawn = true       # Send automatically
stale_threshold_hours = 24        # Ignore context older than this

## Scope
- Add RecoveryConfig struct to internal/config/
- Parse from config.toml and environment variables
- Document options in example config
- Support per-session overrides via flags

## Acceptance Criteria
- All options configurable via config file
- Environment variable overrides work
- --no-recovery flag disables for single spawn
- Defaults are sensible (all enabled)

## Technical Notes

 {"id":"bd-1nqde","title":"Task: Parity Gate CI (kernel coverage + CLI/REST parity)","description":"## Scope\nAdd CI tests that prevent drift between CLI, robot mode, and REST/OpenAPI.\n\n## Implementation\n- Validate every kernel command has CLI + REST bindings.\n- Ensure every CLI command is registered in kernel.\n- Compare generated OpenAPI vs checked‑in spec (fail on diff).\n- CLI vs REST parity tests for representative commands.\n\n## Logging\n- Log missing bindings/commands with exact IDs and paths.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: kernel coverage tests.\n- Integration: parity harness comparing normalized CLI JSON vs REST JSON.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- CI fails on missing bindings or OpenAPI drift.\n- Parity tests catch output mismatches deterministically.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:37.823284186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757982074Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T20:24:55.757424424Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-204ic","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nqde","depends_on_id":"bd-vmwdj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ny8s","title":"Task: Write E2E tests for ensemble Robot API","description":"# Task: Robot API E2E Tests

End-to-end tests for ensemble robot commands with full JSON validation.

## Coverage
- --robot-ensemble
- --robot-ensemble-spawn
- --robot-ensemble-synthesize
- --robot-ensemble-modes (default core)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --tier=all (includes advanced)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --category=Formal (filter)
- --robot-ensemble-modes --limit/--offset (pagination)
- --robot-ensemble-presets (list)
- **advanced gating**: spawn with advanced mode without `--allow-advanced` (expect error), then with `--allow-advanced` (expect success)

## Logging Requirements
- Log raw JSON output
- Log parsed response structs
- Log session name + agent counts
- Log tier counts (core vs advanced)
- Log error_code + hint for gating failures

 {"id":"bd-1o1q1","title":"RCH: Add --robot-rch-status and --robot-rch-workers commands","description":"Add robot mode commands for RCH status and worker information.

## Command: --robot-rch-status

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-status
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "rch": {
    "enabled": true,
    "available": true,
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "workers": {
      "total": 3,
      "healthy": 2,
      "busy": 1
    },
    "session_stats": {
      "builds_total": 45,
      "builds_remote": 30,
      "builds_local": 15,
      "time_saved_seconds": 1200
    }
  }
}
```

## Command: --robot-rch-workers

```bash
ntm --robot-rch-workers [--worker=NAME]
```

### Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "workers": [
    {
      "name": "builder-1",
      "host": "192.168.1.100",
      "status": "healthy",
      "current_build": null,
      "builds_completed": 20,
      "cpu_percent": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "builder-2",
      "host": "192.168.1.101",
      "status": "busy",
      "current_build": "cargo build --release",
      "builds_completed": 25,
      "cpu_percent": 95
    }
  ]
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "rch not found in PATH",
  "hint": "Install rch: cargo install rch"
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/robot/rch_status.go
- internal/robot/rch_workers.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-rch-status` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--robot-rch-workers` flag registered in root.go
- [ ] `--worker=NAME` filter works for specific worker
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly
- [ ] Missing rch returns actionable error with install hint
- [ ] Workers array includes all configured workers
- [ ] session_stats aggregates from current session
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on partial data, 2 on error

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/rch_status_test.go)
- [ ] Flag parsing for all options
- [ ] rch status parsing
- [ ] rch workers parsing
- [ ] JSON output serialization
- [ ] Error handling for missing rch
- [ ] Error handling for unreachable workers

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_rch.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-RCH] Robot RCH Status Tests
set -euo pipefail
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-RCH]"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX Starting robot RCH E2E tests"

# Check if rch is available
if \! command -v rch &>/dev/null; then
    echo "$LOG_PREFIX SKIP: rch not installed"
    exit 0
fi

# Test 1: RCH status
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 1: RCH status"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-status)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".rch.version") \!= "" ]] || exit 1

# Test 2: RCH workers
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 2: RCH workers"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-rch-workers)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Result: $RESULT"
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r ".success") == "true" ]] || exit 1
[[ $(echo "$RESULT" | jq ".workers | type") == "\"array\"" ]] || exit 1

# Test 3: Missing rch fallback (if applicable)
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Test 3: Error handling"
# Mock test - verify error schema
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Error schema verified"

echo "$LOG_PREFIX All robot RCH E2E tests passed"
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:54.549811488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T17:53:32.790300Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1o1q1","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o1q1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ghcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1o1q1","depends_on_id":"bd-3u84u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1o2r","title":"Fix double-width character handling (emoji, CJK)","description":"## Problem
Double-width characters (emoji, CJK, some Unicode) cause column misalignment because they take 2 terminal cells but count as 1 rune.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Good News: Already Handled Correctly

**The codebase uses `lipgloss.Width()` which internally uses `go-runewidth`:**

1. **table.go:205-210** - `runeWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// runeWidth returns the visual display width of a string.
// Uses lipgloss.Width() which properly handles ANSI escape codes and
// double-width characters (CJK, emoji) that occupy 2 terminal columns.
func runeWidth(s string) int {
    return lipgloss.Width(s)
}
```

2. **layout.go:93-128** - `TruncateWidth()` uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// TruncateWidth trims a string to fit within maxWidth terminal columns,
// ...Unlike TruncateRunes, this uses lipgloss.Width()
// to properly account for double-width characters (CJK, emoji) and ANSI codes.
func TruncateWidth(s string, maxWidth int, suffix string) string {
    currentWidth := lipgloss.Width(s)
    ...
}
```

3. **badges.go:687-712** - truncation uses `lipgloss.Width()`:
```go
// Uses lipgloss.Width() to properly handle ANSI codes and double-width runes.
```

### Non-Width Uses Are Correct

Found `utf8.RuneCountInString()` used in:
- **activity.go:81-82** - Counting characters for velocity metrics (chars/sec)
- **palette/model.go:1279** - Displaying "X chars" badge

Both are for CHARACTER COUNTING, not display width. Using `RuneCountInString` is correct there.

## Verification Tests Required

Add tests to verify double-width handling:

```go
func TestDoubleWidthCharacterWidth(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        input    string
        wantWidth int
    }{
        {"ASCII", "hello", 5},
        {"Emoji single", "😀", 2},
        {"Emoji sequence", "👨‍👩‍👧‍👦", 2},  // Family emoji (ZWJ sequence)
        {"CJK", "中文", 4},
        {"Mixed", "hi中文!", 7},
        {"Checkmark", "✓", 1},
        {"Warning", "⚠", 1},
        {"Full block", "█", 1},
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            got := lipgloss.Width(tc.input)
            if got != tc.wantWidth {
                t.Errorf("Width(%q) = %d, want %d", tc.input, got, tc.wantWidth)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestTableAlignmentWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    table := NewStyledTable("Status", "Name", "Count")
    table.AddRow("✓", "Test 1", "10")
    table.AddRow("⚠", "Test 2 中文", "20")
    table.AddRow("✗", "Test 😀 3", "30")
    
    rendered := table.Render()
    lines := strings.Split(rendered, "\n")
    
    // All lines should have same width (accounting for borders)
    var widths []int
    for _, line := range lines {
        if strings.TrimSpace(line) != "" {
            widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(line))
        }
    }
    
    for i := 1; i < len(widths); i++ {
        if widths[i] != widths[0] {
            t.Errorf("line %d width %d != first line width %d", i, widths[i], widths[0])
        }
    }
}

func TestTruncateWidthWithEmoji(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input    string
        maxWidth int
        want     string
    }{
        {"hello😀world", 8, "hello😀…"},  // emoji is 2 wide
        {"中文测试", 5, "中文…"},          // CJK truncation
        {"test中", 5, "test…"},           // Mixed truncation
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        got := layout.TruncateWidth(tc.input, tc.maxWidth, "…")
        gotWidth := lipgloss.Width(got)
        if gotWidth > tc.maxWidth {
            t.Errorf("TruncateWidth(%q, %d) = %q (width %d), exceeds max", 
                tc.input, tc.maxWidth, got, gotWidth)
        }
    }
}
```

## E2E Visual Test (VHS)
```tape
# double_width_test.tape
Output double_width_test.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200

Type "ntm --demo-mode"
Sleep 2s

# The demo should show agents with emoji icons
# Verify alignment visually
Screenshot double_width_check.png
```

## Status: Verification Only

The implementation is already correct. This bead becomes a verification/documentation task:
- [x] runeWidth() uses lipgloss.Width()
- [x] TruncateWidth() uses lipgloss.Width() 
- [x] Non-width uses are appropriate (char counting)
- [ ] Add unit tests for double-width scenarios
- [ ] Verify in VHS recording

## Priority Adjustment  
Changed to P3 - verification task.  {"id":"bd-3954","title":"Task: Create FileReservationWatcher module","description":"## Purpose
Create a background watcher that monitors pane output and auto-reserves files.

## Module Location
internal/watcher/file_reservation.go

## FileReservationWatcher Struct
type FileReservationWatcher struct {
    client             *agentmail.Client
    projectDir         string
    activeReservations map[string]time.Time  // pane -> last activity
    mu                 sync.Mutex
}

## Key Methods
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) Start(ctx context.Context)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) checkPaneOutputs()
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) OnFileEdit(pane Pane, files []string)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) releaseIdleReservations()

## Watcher Loop
- Poll every 10 seconds
- For each pane, capture recent output
- Parse for file edit patterns
- Reserve new files detected
- Release reservations for idle panes (10m+ inactive)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background watcher starts with session
- Detects file edits from pane output
- Reserves files automatically in Agent Mail
- Releases reservations on idle
- Thread-safe operation

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context for lifecycle
Coordinate with session lifecycle events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:19.522299399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901182127Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901094762Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3954","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-396n1","title":"process_triage: Add dashboard health indicators per agent","description":"Add health status indicators to agent entries in dashboard.

## Design

### Indicator Placement
Next to each agent/pane name in the dashboard.

### Visual States
- 🟢 useful/waiting: Agent is working or legitimately waiting
- 🟡 idle (>5 min): Agent finished, no new work
- 🔴 stuck (>10 min): Agent appears unresponsive
- ⚫ zombie: Process not running properly

### Enhanced Pane List
```
┌─ Agents ───────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🟢 agent-1   claude-code    Working (5m)       │
│ 🟡 agent-2   codex          Idle (8m)          │
│ 🔴 agent-3   gemini         Stuck (15m) ⚠️     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Alert Behavior
When agent is stuck:
- Indicator turns red
- Optional alert badge (⚠️)
- Click to see details/options

## Implementation

```go
func (m *DashboardModel) renderAgentStatus(pane string) string {
    state := m.healthMonitor.GetState(pane)
    
    switch state.Classification {
    case "useful", "waiting":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("green")).Render("🟢")
    case "idle":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("yellow")).Render("🟡")
    case "stuck":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("red")).Render("🔴")
    default:
        return "⚫"
    }
}
```

-{"id":"bd-397f0","title":"Feature: Observability Metrics - Coverage, redundancy, velocity","description":"# Feature: Observability Metrics

Real-time and post-run metrics for understanding ensemble effectiveness.

## Metrics Dashboard

### Coverage Map
Visual display of which reasoning categories were invoked:
```
Category Coverage:
[A] Formal      ██░░ 2/4 modes
[B] Ampliative  ███░ 3/5 modes
[C] Uncertainty ░░░░ 0/3 modes
[E] Causal      ██░░ 2/4 modes
[F] Dialectical █░░ 1/3 modes
...

Blind Spots: Uncertainty reasoning (C), Modal reasoning (J)
```

### Redundancy Score
How similar were the outputs?
```
Redundancy Analysis:
Overall Score: 0.34 (acceptable)

Pairwise Similarity:
F1 ↔ E2: 0.23 (low - good diversity)
F1 ↔ K1: 0.67 (high - overlapping insights)
E2 ↔ L3: 0.18 (low - good diversity)

Recommendation: Consider replacing K1 with different mode
```

### Findings Velocity
Insights per token spent:
```
Findings Velocity:
Overall: 2.3 findings / 1k tokens

Per Mode:
F1 Argument Mapping:    3.1 findings/1k (high value)
E2 Root Cause:          2.8 findings/1k
K1 Systems Thinking:    1.2 findings/1k (low value)
L3 Meta-cognitive:      2.5 findings/1k
H2 Strategic:           1.9 findings/1k

Suggestion: K1 underperforming, consider early stop
```

### Conflict Density
Disagreements per output pair:
```
Conflict Analysis:
Total Conflicts: 7
Resolved: 4
Unresolved: 3

High-Conflict Pairs:
F1 ↔ E2: 3 conflicts (different root cause theories)
K1 ↔ H2: 2 conflicts (scope disagreement)

Action: Review unresolved conflicts in synthesis report
```

## Post-Run Report

After ensemble completes, generate metrics report:
- Which modes provided most value (findings/token)
- Which modes were redundant
- Coverage gaps to address in future runs
- Budget efficiency score

## Data Sources
- Use **normalized ModeOutput** from OutputCollector / SchemaValidator (no raw parsing)
- Token counts from BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator
- Conflict density prefers DisagreementAuditor output when available; fall back to heuristic-only if auditor not run

## Determinism & Performance
- All metrics are deterministic and cacheable per run
- Stable ordering for UI (category order A..L, mode order by assignment)
- Avoid heavy NLP; prefer normalized text + evidence pointers

## Logging Requirements
- Log metric calculation inputs (mode IDs + counts)
- Log threshold crossings (high redundancy, low velocity, zero coverage)
- Log when conflict density uses auditor vs. fallback

## Files
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Core metric calculations
- internal/ensemble/coverage.go - Category coverage tracking
- internal/ensemble/similarity.go - Output similarity detection
- internal/ensemble/velocity.go - Findings velocity tracking
- internal/ensemble/conflicts.go - Conflict density
- internal/tui/metrics_panel.go - Dashboard visualization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Metrics computed from normalized outputs and token counts
- [ ] Deterministic results across runs with same inputs
- [ ] Thresholds produce actionable suggestions
- [ ] Unit tests for each metric + E2E (bd-3sl6a)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:58:30.938531481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:43:03.214274845Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-397f0","depends_on_id":"bd-1av7i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-397f0","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-397f0","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-397f0","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4tl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-397f0","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-398bh","title":"RCH: Implement PreToolUse hook configuration for build interception","description":"Configure PreToolUse hook for build command interception.

## Purpose

When an agent runs a build command, intercept and offload to RCH.

## Hook Configuration

```toml
[[hooks.PreToolUse]]
name = "rch-intercept"
command = "rch intercept"
patterns = [
  "^cargo build",
  "^cargo rustc",
  "^go build"
]
```

## Behavior

1. Agent invokes: `cargo build --release`
2. Hook matches pattern
3. Transforms to: `rch intercept cargo build --release`
4. RCH decides: offload or local

## Acceptance Criteria

1. PreToolUse hook configured for cargo build
2. Cargo rustc commands intercepted
3. Transparent offload to rch when available
4. Fallback to local build when rch unavailable
5. Config option in [integrations.rch]
6. Unit tests for hook configuration
7. E2E test for full build interception","acceptance_criteria":"1. PreToolUse hook configured for cargo build
2. Cargo rustc commands intercepted
3. Transparent offload to rch when available
4. Fallback to local build when rch unavailable
5. Config option in [integrations.rch]
6. Unit tests for hook configuration
7. E2E test for full build interception","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:46.573990254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:10:56.542532707Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-398bh","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-398bh","depends_on_id":"bd-1ghcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-398bh","depends_on_id":"bd-3u84u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39lt4","title":"Task: Implement CoverageMap metric for reasoning category analysis","description":"# Task: CoverageMap Implementation

Track which reasoning categories were invoked and identify blind spots.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/coverage.go
type CoverageMap struct {
    Categories map[ModeCategory]CategoryCoverage
}

type CategoryCoverage struct {
    Category   ModeCategory
    TotalModes int
    UsedModes  []string
    Coverage   float64 // used/total
}

type CoverageReport struct {
    Overall     float64  // categories with at least 1 mode / total categories
    PerCategory map[ModeCategory]CategoryCoverage
    BlindSpots  []ModeCategory // categories with 0 coverage
    Suggestions []string       // recommendations for better coverage
}

func NewCoverageMap(catalog *ModeCatalog) *CoverageMap
func (c *CoverageMap) RecordMode(modeID string)
func (c *CoverageMap) CalculateCoverage() *CoverageReport
func (c *CoverageMap) Render() string // TUI-friendly output
```

## Determinism
- Use stable category ordering (A..L) for Render output
- Use catalog metadata for category membership (no ad-hoc tags)
- Suggestions should be deterministic (pick lowest-cost/core modes that fill blind spots)

## UI Representation
```
Category Coverage:
[A] Formal      ██░░ 2/4 modes
[B] Ampliative  ███░ 3/5 modes
[C] Uncertainty ░░░░ 0/3 modes
...
Blind Spots: Uncertainty reasoning (C), Modal reasoning (J)
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log per-category counts (total vs used)
- Log detected blind spots and suggested modes

## Tests
- Unit: coverage calculation + blind spot detection + deterministic ordering
- Fixtures: catalog with mixed category coverage

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Track mode usage by category
- [ ] Calculate overall and per-category coverage
- [ ] Identify blind spots (zero-coverage categories)
- [ ] Generate actionable suggestions
- [ ] TUI-friendly render method","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T18:40:58.694810397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:34:31.292459643Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:34:31.292385475Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39lt4","depends_on_id":"bd-397f0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39uo","title":"Task: Implement interactive Bubble Tea wizard","description":"## Purpose
Create an interactive setup wizard for non-technical users using Bubble Tea TUI framework.

## Wizard Flow
1. Project type selection (Go/Python/Node/Rust/Other)
2. Agent count configuration
3. Integration toggles (Agent Mail, CASS, CM)
4. File reservation auto-enable toggle
5. Confirmation and summary

## UI Components
- Selection list for project type
- Number input for agent count
- Toggle/checkbox for integrations
- Progress indicator during setup
- Summary display at end

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wizard runs by default, --non-interactive skips it
- All options configurable through wizard
- Escape key exits cleanly
- Summary shows all chosen options before confirming

## Technical Notes
Use existing Bubble Tea patterns from internal/tui/
Match dashboard styling (Catppuccin theme)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:31:55.603636143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578235604Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578151726Z","close_reason":"Interactive Bubble Tea wizard already implemented in init_wizard.go","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39uo","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
